title: valeriana officinalis subsp. sambucifolia
intro: Valeriana officinalis subsp. sambucifolia, also known as valerian or valerian root, is a subspecies of the valerian plant (Valeriana officinalis) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant is renowned for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its sedative and anti-anxiety effects, which are attributed to the presence of valerenic acid and isovaleric acid. The health benefits of valerian root include improved sleep quality, reduced stress and anxiety, and relief from symptoms of menopause, depression, and restlessness. Valerian root is commonly consumed in herbal preparations, such as teas, capsules, tinctures, and extracts, which are available in various forms and can be used to promote relaxation and well-being.
